headline: Nola and Harper give the Phillies a real foothold
summary_text: The Phillies are still digging out at 15-20, fourth in the NL East and 9.5 games back, but the last week finally changed the shape of the conversation. Monday's 1-0 win in Miami gave them a series win, a 7-3 last-10 mark and a two-game winning streak, with Bryce Harper supplying the only run on his seventh homer and Aaron Nola answering with six scoreless innings and no walks. The larger profile remains uneven: Philadelphia is carrying a -42 run differential, a .667 OPS and a 4.61 team ERA, so this is not a clean turnaround yet. It is, however, the first stretch in a while where the pitching has given the lineup enough margin, and the upcoming Athletics series at Citizens Bank Park gives the Phillies a practical chance to turn a rebound into something more durable.

key_stats.label: Power leaders
key_stats.value: Kyle Schwarber 11 HR; Bryce Harper 7 HR

key_stats.label: Pitching leaders
key_stats.value: Jesus Luzardo 51 K; Cristopher Sanchez 2.90 ERA
